proposedAction: Engine matches fixture but the upstream-axios patch round-trip blows up (post-patch matches=11) because the rule sink pattern is too broad and fires on `test/unit/adapters/http.js`. F3 engine work: add a `file_glob_exclude` or `test_path_filter` knob in `validate.ts`'s patch round-trip so test files are not counted as production reachability. Alternatively widen the AI prompt to require sink-side request-config-arg checks for axios SSRF (CVE-specific).

Vuln is header value injection via prototype pollution: there is no user input source in the fixture — `Object.prototype` mutation is the source-of-evil. Taint engine cannot model this. F4: route to a non-taint detector (presence-of-vulnerable-version + presence-of-Object.prototype-mutation) or skip this CVE class. Engine flow = 0 even with extended specs is correct.

Bundled `lodash.yaml` already emits one `_.template(*)` flow on this fixture (vuln_class=code_injection). E2 (engine computed-key / dynamic-property taint) should flip this — currently the engine taints the value side of `userImports[req.query.maliciousKey] = ...` but does not taint the OBJECT when an attacker-controlled KEY is assigned. Sink fires only after engine recognises the keyed-write as a taint sink (see `propagate-core.ts` member-write handling).

Weak/mismatched algorithm-vs-key vulnerability. AI rule almost certainly uses vuln_class=`weak_crypto`/`auth_bypass`. E3 enum extension lets the schema accept these classes. Also needs F1 jsonwebtoken framework_model: add `jwt.sign(*)` and `jwt.verify(*)` sinks with vuln_class=auth_bypass, taint-kind file/literal for key arg.

Engine fires on bundled JSON.parse sink (vuln_class=deserialization), but CVE is config-injection via `axios.mergeConfig`. Add `axios.mergeConfig(*)` sink to a new `axios.yaml` framework_model with vuln_class=prototype_pollution. AI generated rule emits the right intent but the bundled sink hides the real one in widening. Either move axios sources/sinks out of express widening OR add `axios.mergeConfig` to bundled axios spec.

ReDoS in the debug `%o` formatter — taint flows correctly to a winston bundled sink (widened on `log_injection`) but Gate-3 patch round-trip fails because the upstream fix is in `src/common.js` regex code, not in any caller. F4: this is a parser-internal ReDoS; the taint regime can flag the bundled `log(*)` sink but the patch round-trip needs to widen to fix-file-only and skip per-caller post-match counting.

Add `semver.yaml` framework_model with sinks `new semver.Range(*)`, `semver.validRange(*)`, `semver.satisfies(*, *)` (arg index 1) vuln_class=redos. Engine sees `req.query.range` source from express but no semver sink is bundled. One-spec add; CVE-2024-29041 already validates on similar pattern.

vulnerableFixturePreview: const KEYUTIL = require('jsrsasign').KEYUTIL; const RSAKey = require('jsrsasign').RSAKey; const express = require('express'); const app = express(); app.use(express.json()); app.post('/decrypt', (req, res) => { const privateKeyPem = `-----B
proposedAction: RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5 Bleichenbacher-style padding-oracle in jsrsasign. AI fixture uses express source. F1: add `jsrsasign.yaml` with sinks `*.RSAKey(*).decrypt(*)`, `*.KEYUTIL.getKey(*)` vuln_class=weak_crypto (needs E3 enum extension). Borderline F4 if AI generates `padding_oracle` — but framing as weak_crypto is reasonable.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load)
proposedAction: vuln_class=`header_leak` (cross-origin Authorization-header leak on redirect). E3 enum extension: add `auth_bypass` and let aliaser fold `header_leak` → `auth_bypass`. F1: add `follow-redirects.yaml` with HTTP-response-header sinks would not help — this is a library-internal cross-redirect propagation bug. Realistic bucket: F4 non-taint (presence-of-vulnerable-version + presence-of-Authorization-header in fixture).

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load)
proposedAction: vuln_class=`insecure_default` (Session.verify=False persisting). F4 non-taint: configuration-flag check. Add a `requests.Session(verify=False)` rule to the bundled `requests.yaml` framework_model but route through a non-taint config-detector regime, OR add `insecure_default` to the enum and alias it to `weak_crypto`. CVE pattern is well-known: 1 LOC AST check.

vulnerableFixturePreview:
from flask import Flask, request
import urllib3
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/fetch')
def fetch():
    method = request.args.get('method')
    conn = urllib3.HTTPConnection('example.com')
    conn.putrequest(method, '/')
proposedAction: Engine fires on bundled `*.putrequest(*)` urllib3 sink (post Phase ABCD `urllib3.yaml`). But validation fails because patch round-trip can't find pre-patch matches in `src/urllib3/connection.py`. F3 engine: the AI's sink pattern likely uses `putrequest(method=*)` keyword form; engine `*.putrequest(*)` matches positional only. Add keyword-arg matching to `matchesCallPattern` (engine F3 feature).

vulnerableFixturePreview:
from flask import Flask, request
import cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization.pkcs7 as pkcs7
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/verify', methods=['POST'])
def verify():
    data = request.get_data()
    certificates = pkcs7.load_d
proposedAction: NULL-pointer deref in PKCS7 cert loading. Not a taint vuln — any caller with attacker bytes crashes. F4: this is a memory-safety bug in a C extension. Static taint cannot meaningfully classify; presence-detection (calls `pkcs7.load_*` with attacker bytes) is the best we can do and is essentially what taint already enables. Engine flow=0 because no bundled pkcs7 sink. Add `pkcs7.load_*` sink as `dos` once F4 enum extension lands.

vulnerableFixturePreview: from flask import Flask, request import jinja2 template = "" def render_template(attrs): env = jinja2.Environment() tmpl = env.from_string(template) return tmpl.render(attrs=attrs) app = Flask(__
proposedAction: jinja2 `xmlattr` filter XSS. Phase ABCD added `jinja2.yaml` with `*.from_string(*)` + `*.render(*)` sinks, but here the template literal is constant and attacker control is in `attrs=attrs` keyword arg — engine F3 work: kwarg taint into `render` call. The vulnerability is the kwarg-to-template-context flow. Engine needs keyword-arg-aware sink matching + cross-function call-edge taint (currently only positional propagation).

vulnerableFixturePreview:
from flask import Flask, request
import jinja2
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/render')
def render():
    template_str = request.args.get('template')
    env = jinja2.SandboxedEnvironment()
    template = env.from_string(template_str)
proposedAction: Sandbox escape via `str.format_map`. Engine already fires `*.from_string(*)` sink (code_injection) on the bundled jinja2 spec. Validation likely fails because AI labeled vuln_class=`sandbox_escape` and widening canonicalises only to known classes. E1 vuln_class normalization should fold sandbox_escape → code_injection.

ReDoS in jinja2 `urlize` filter. Engine fires `*.render(*)` with vuln_class=xss (jinja2 spec default). AI rule labeled as `redos` — E1 vuln_class normalization needs `redos` ⇄ `xss` widening when same sink fires, OR add explicit `redos` sink for `*.render(*)` when template contains `|urlize`. Realistically the bundled sink class mismatch is the issue.

Engine fires `ImageMath.eval(*)` sink on bundled pillow spec. Failure is Gate-3 patch round-trip: upstream patch is in `src/PIL/ImageMath.py` adding `expression=` keyword-only parsing; per-file matcher counts old positional-call test fixtures as still-vulnerable. F3 engine: file-glob filter for `tests/` in patch round-trip + keyword-vs-positional canonicalisation.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=oob_read)
proposedAction: Out-of-bounds read in BLP decoder (C extension). Memory-safety in native code; static analysis on Python source cannot model. UNCOVERABLE under the taint regime. Best F4: presence-of-vulnerable-version detector + Pillow.Image.open called with attacker bytes — but this is true for the entire library surface and yields huge FP rate.

vulnerableFixturePreview:
from flask import Flask, request, session
app = Flask(__name__)
app.secret_key = 'secret'
@app.route('/login')
def login():
    session['user_id'] = 1
    session.permanent = True
    return ''
proposedAction: Caching proxy can serve flask session cookie to another user when `SESSION_REFRESH_EACH_REQUEST` + cache headers misalign. Not a taint vuln. F4: AST config-detector — flag `session.permanent=True` without explicit `Vary` / `private` cache header. UNCOVERABLE under static taint; doable via config rule.

Certifi shipped E-Tugra root in its CA bundle; remediation = upgrade certifi. There is no source-level call pattern that distinguishes vulnerable usage from safe usage — every `certifi.where()` / `certifi.contents()` caller is equally affected. UNCOVERABLE under any source-level static analysis. Detect via package-version only.

vulnerableFixturePreview:
from flask import Flask, request
import setuptools.package_index
app = Flask(__name__)
@app.route('/install')
def install():
    url = request.args.get('package_url')
    index = setuptools.package_index.PackageIndex()
    return index.d
proposedAction: RCE via setuptools `PackageIndex.download` to attacker-controlled URL (svn/git/hg shell-out). F1 coverage: add `setuptools.yaml` framework_model with sinks `*.PackageIndex().download(*)`, `*.PackageIndex().fetch_distribution(*)` vuln_class=command_injection. Source already detected (flask).

Spring4Shell — `BeanWrapper.setPropertyValues` with attacker-controlled `class.module.classLoader.*`. F1 coverage: add to `spring-boot.yaml` (or a new `spring-beans.yaml`) sinks `*.setPropertyValue*(*)`, `*.BeanWrapperImpl(*)` vuln_class=code_injection. Source needed: `@RequestParam` already in `spring-boot.yaml`.

RegexRequestMatcher authorization bypass. E3 (auth_bypass enum) + F1 spring-security framework_model. New `spring-security.yaml` with sinks `new RegexRequestMatcher(*)`, `*.regexMatchers(*)` vuln_class=auth_bypass. Source = MaliciousMatcher constructor; non-trivial because the bug is matcher-construction not HTTP-request flow.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=dos)
proposedAction: Spring Boot Observation metrics DoS via unbounded tag cardinality. F4 non-taint: requires reasoning about cardinality of an HTTP-derived label, not a single taint flow. UNCOVERABLE under taint regime. Detect via config-rule: `Observation.start(name, KeyValues.of(*, request.*))` with HTTP-input in the value position is suspicious — but extremely high FP.

XML signature private-key disclosure via DTM debug logging. E4 work is adding `xmlsec.yaml` (or `santuario.yaml`) framework_model with sinks `*.XMLSignature(*)`, `*.checkSignatureValue(*)` vuln_class=information_disclosure (needs E3 enum extension). Source: `@RequestBody`. If E4 lands xmlsec, this validates.

Log4j 1.x (not 2.x!) deserialization via SocketAppender / HardenedObjectInputStream. F1 coverage: bundled `log4j.yaml` covers log4j-2 patterns. Add log4j-1.x deserialization sinks `new SocketAppender(*)`, `*.startServer(*)` vuln_class=deserialization. Source: `@RequestBody` already detected.

Apache POI ZIP-bomb / XML-expansion DoS in HWPF parser. F1 coverage: add `apache-poi.yaml` framework_model with sinks `new HWPFDocument(*)`, `WorkbookFactory.create(*)`, `new XSSFWorkbook(*)` vuln_class=dos (needs E3 enum extension for dos). Source `byte[] data` parameter — engine F3 needed for parameter-as-source.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=dos)
proposedAction: Accept-Language header parsing DoS. E3 enum extension (`dos`) + F1 add `x-text.yaml` with sinks `language.ParseAcceptLanguage(*)`. Source = http Request header. Borderline F4 (resource exhaustion) but the call pattern is concrete enough to flag.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=denial_of_service)
proposedAction: HTTP/2 GOAWAY frame DoS — protocol-level bug in golang.org/x/net/http2. UNCOVERABLE under taint: there is no caller-side pattern; any program serving HTTP/2 with a vulnerable version is affected. Detect via package-version only. E3 normalization (`denial_of_service` → `dos`) lets the rule pass schema but engine emits no flows.

XSS via foreign-content namespace confusion in golang.org/x/net/html. F1 coverage: add `x-net-html.yaml` framework_model with sinks `html.Parse(*)`, `html.ParseFragment(*)` vuln_class=xss. Source: untrusted bytes — engine F3 needed for byte-slice-as-source. Without a Gin/Echo source the fixture has no flow.
HTTP/2 Rapid Reset (CVE-2023-44487). Protocol-level DoS in any HTTP/2 server. AI fixture is nonsense (imports `io/netty` in Go). UNCOVERABLE under static taint; package-version only.

Misuse of `ServerConfig.PublicKeyCallback` — callers store key across calls assuming uniqueness. F1 coverage: add `x-crypto-ssh.yaml` with sinks `ssh.ServerConfig{PublicKeyCallback: *}` vuln_class=auth_bypass. Hard to express as a call-pattern sink — closer to a struct-field-init AST rule. Borderline F3 (struct-field sink type) or F4 (config check).
runc container escape via leaked file descriptor (`/proc/self/fd/$N`). Container-runtime bug; no application-level call pattern triggers it. UNCOVERABLE under static taint; package-version only.

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=uncontrolled_resource_consumption)
proposedAction: Prometheus HTTP handler unbounded label cardinality DoS. E3 enum extension: alias `uncontrolled_resource_consumption` → `dos`. F1: add `prometheus-client.yaml` with sinks `*.HandlerFor(*)`, `*.WithLabelValues(*)` vuln_class=dos, but the real bug is HTTP method label cardinality which is a config-rule pattern not a single-flow.

Consul HTTP health-check follows redirects to internal services (SSRF-like). Application calls `checks.NewHttpCheck`; the vuln is consul-internal. UNCOVERABLE in caller code — only package-version detection works. F4: at most flag presence of `checks.NewHttp*`. Engine emits 0 flows because no http source in fixture (httptest is not bundled).

vulnerableFixturePreview:
require 'action_dispatch'
class LeakController
  def call(env)
    request = ActionDispatch::Request.new(env)
    response = ActionDispatch::Response.create(200, {}, ['leaky body'])
    ActionDispatch::Executor.wrap do
      response.body
proposedAction: Rails response body leak across requests in fiber-local executor wrapping. F1 coverage: add `actionpack.yaml` (or extend `rails.yaml`) with sinks `*.ActionDispatch::Executor.wrap(*)` vuln_class=information_disclosure. Source: env-derived state. Engine F3 needed for ruby block-arg taint propagation.

vulnerableFixturePreview:
require 'active_support/core_ext/string/output_safety'
string = "hello".html_safe
user_input = params[:content]
string.bytesplice(1, 0, user_input)
proposedAction: ActiveSupport SafeBuffer XSS via `bytesplice` mutating an html_safe string with attacker input. F1: add `*.bytesplice(*)` sink to ruby-stdlib spec vuln_class=xss. F3 engine: ruby `params[:content]` source — bundled `rails.yaml` should already cover `params.*`; verify ruby propagator recognises the bracket-access form `params[:content]` (vs `params.content`).

vulnerableFixturePreview:

git = Git.open('evil')
git.clone('evil', 'clone1')
proposedAction: git gem command injection via crafted repo path argument. F1 coverage: add `ruby-git.yaml` framework_model with sinks `Git.open(*)`, `Git.clone(*)`, `*.git.clone(*)` vuln_class=command_injection. Source: file/argv (engine F2 source-coverage needed — fixture has no http source).

vulnerableFixturePreview: (empty — rejected at spec_load with vuln_class=resource_exhaustion)
proposedAction: Rack multipart parser DoS via crafted Content-Type. E3 enum extension: alias `resource_exhaustion` → `dos`. F1: add `Rack::Multipart.parse_multipart(*)` sink to bundled `rails.yaml` or new `rack.yaml` framework_model vuln_class=dos. Source = http body. Realistically a parser-internal DoS — F4 borderline.
